RECIST PR in 21% for measurable disease. CTC clearance 58%. Median rPFS 4.8 months. dnTGFbRII resulted in 4x higher tumor infiltration vs prior non-armored PSMA CAR-T. CRS mild (grade 1-2 in 42%). Xerostomia (salivary gland PSMA expression) in 25%.
outcome_summary: PSA50 decline 33%, PR 21%, 4x higher tumor infiltration with armoring

title: CD5-Targeted CAR-T with CD5 Knockout for Relapsed/Refractory T-Cell Lymphoma
text_summary: Phase I of CD5-directed CAR-T with CRISPR-mediated CD5 knockout to prevent fratricide in 12 patients with R/R peripheral T-cell lymphoma (PTCL). CD5 knockout efficiency 88-95%. ORR 58% (7/12): 3 CR, 4 PR. Median follow-up 9 months. CRS 67% (grade ≥3: 8%). Partial T-cell depletion (CD5+ subset eliminated, CD5- T-cells preserved). Less immunosuppressive than CD7 CAR-T. First dedicated CAR-T trial for PTCL showing meaningful activity.
outcome_summary: ORR 58%, 3 CR, fratricide-resistant, partial T-cell preservation

title: CD33-Targeted CAR-T in Relapsed/Refractory AML
text_summary: Phase I of CD33-directed CAR-T in 18 patients with R/R AML. CD33 widely expressed on AML blasts but also on normal myeloid progenitors. CR/CRi 33% (6/18). Severe myelosuppression universal (grade 4 neutropenia 89%, median duration 28 days). 3 patients bridged to transplant. CRS 72% (grade ≥3: 11%). Demonstrates CD33 CAR-T can achieve remissions but significant on-target myeloid toxicity limits standalone use. Bridge-to-transplant strategy essential.
outcome_summary: CR/CRi 33%, severe myelosuppression universal, bridge-to-transplant

title: FLT3-Targeted CAR-T for Relapsed/Refractory AML with FLT3 Mutations
text_summary: Phase I of FLT3-directed CAR-T in 14 patients with R/R AML harboring FLT3 mutations (ITD or TKD). FLT3 expression on AML blasts high (>80% in mutated cases). CR/CRi 43% (6/14). MRD negativity in 4 responders. Myelosuppression grade ≥3 in 71% but less severe than CD33 targeting (FLT3 less broadly expressed on normal myeloid cells). CRS 57% (grade ≥3: 7%). Synergy potential with FLT3 inhibitors (gilteritinib) to upregulate target expression.
outcome_summary: CR/CRi 43%, FLT3 mutation enriched, FLT3i synergy potential

title: CD30-Targeted CAR-T for Relapsed/Refractory Hodgkin Lymphoma
text_summary: Phase I/II of CD30-directed CAR-T in 41 patients with R/R Hodgkin lymphoma after brentuximab vedotin and checkpoint inhibitor failure. ORR 72% (CR 59%). 2-year PFS 48%. CRS 24% (all grade 1-2, no grade ≥3) — notably mild toxicity profile. No ICANS. CD30 restricted expression (activated lymphocytes, Reed-Sternberg cells) yields favorable safety. Demonstrates CAR-T as effective salvage option for HL after both ADC and checkpoint failure.
outcome_summary: ORR 72%, CR 59%, CRS all grade 1-2, exceptionally mild toxicity

title: CD70-Targeted CAR-T in Relapsed/Refractory AML and Renal Cell Carcinoma
text_summary: Phase I/II of CD70-directed CAR-T (PRGN-3006) with membrane-bound IL-15 armoring in R/R AML (n=18) and clear cell RCC (n=12). AML: CR/CRi 39% (7/18). RCC: ORR 25% (3/12, all PR). mbIL-15 enhanced CAR-T persistence (median 90 days vs 14 days for non-armored). CRS 53% (grade ≥3: 3%). CD70 expression both on AML blasts and RCC — dual-indication targeting. First armored CAR-T with membrane-bound cytokine in clinic.
outcome_summary: AML CR/CRi 39%, RCC ORR 25%, mbIL-15 extends persistence to 90 days

title: ELARA: Tisagenlecleucel in Relapsed/Refractory Follicular Lymphoma
text_summary: Phase II of tisa-cel in 98 patients with R/R follicular lymphoma (grade 1-3a) after ≥2 prior lines including anti-CD20 + alkylator. ORR 86.2%, CR 68.1%. 30-month PFS 57.4% — durable responses in indolent lymphoma. CRS 48.5% (grade ≥3: 0%). ICANS 3.1% (grade ≥3: 0%). Remarkably mild toxicity profile in indolent histology. Led to FDA approval of tisa-cel for R/R FL (3L+), expanding CAR-T beyond aggressive lymphoma.
outcome_summary: ORR 86.2%, CR 68.1%, 30mo PFS 57.4%, CRS grade ≥3: 0%

title: TRANSCEND FL: Lisocabtagene Maraleucel in Relapsed/Refractory Follicular Lymphoma and Marginal Zone Lymphoma
text_summary: Phase II of liso-cel in 105 patients with R/R follicular lymphoma (n=84) and marginal zone lymphoma (n=21). FL ORR 95.7%, CR 73.4%. MZL ORR 85.7%, CR 61.9%. 12-month PFS 74.5% for FL, 71.4% for MZL. CRS 58% (grade ≥3: 1%). ICANS 11% (grade ≥3: 2%). Defined composition (1:1 CD4:CD8) showed consistent dosing. Led to FDA approval of liso-cel for R/R FL (3L+) and first CAR-T approval for MZL.
outcome_summary: FL ORR 95.7%, CR 73.4%; MZL ORR 85.7%. First CAR-T for MZL

title: Dual BCMA/CD38 Bispecific CAR-T in Multiple Myeloma After BCMA CAR-T Failure
text_summary: Phase I of novel bispecific CAR-T co-targeting BCMA and CD38 in 18 patients with R/R MM who relapsed after prior BCMA-directed therapy (CAR-T or bispecific antibody). ORR 67% (12/18), CR 39% (7/18). In patients with documented BCMA loss: ORR 50% via CD38-mediated killing. Dual targeting reduced escape: 0/12 responders showed loss of both antigens at relapse. CRS 78% (grade ≥3: 6%). Median follow-up 10 months. Demonstrates dual-antigen approach overcomes single-target resistance in myeloma.
outcome_summary: ORR 67% post BCMA CAR-T failure, 0% dual antigen escape

title: SLAMF7-Targeted CAR-T in Relapsed/Refractory Multiple Myeloma (CARAMBA Project)
text_summary: Phase I/II European CARAMBA project: SLAMF7 (CS1)-directed CAR-T using non-viral Sleeping Beauty transposon system for manufacturing. 12 patients with R/R MM. ORR 50% (6/12), including 2 CR. Non-viral manufacturing successful in 100% (no lentiviral vector needed). Reduced manufacturing cost estimated at 30-50% vs viral vector approaches. CRS 58% (grade ≥3: 0%). SLAMF7 universally expressed on MM cells including BCMA-negative clones. First non-viral transposon CAR-T in myeloma.
outcome_summary: ORR 50%, non-viral Sleeping Beauty transposon, 100% manufacturing success

title: TROP2-Targeted CAR-T in Advanced Triple-Negative Breast Cancer
text_summary: Phase I dose-escalation of TROP2-directed CAR-T in 15 patients with metastatic TNBC after prior sacituzumab govitecan and checkpoint inhibitor. ORR 20% (3/15): 3 PR including 1 durable >6 months. DCR 53% (8/15). CRS 47% (grade ≥3: 0%). Skin toxicity grade 2 in 27% (TROP2 on normal epithelium). Mucosal toxicity (oral/GI) in 20%. CAR-T expansion detectable but modest (solid tumor challenge). Combining with anti-PD-1 planned for next cohort. First TROP2 CAR-T clinical data.
outcome_summary: ORR 20%, DCR 53%, manageable epithelial toxicity, first TROP2 CAR-T data

title: EpCAM-Targeted CAR-T with Intraperitoneal Delivery for Peritoneal Carcinomatosis
text_summary: Phase I evaluating EpCAM-directed CAR-T delivered intraperitoneally in 16 patients with peritoneal carcinomatosis from colorectal (n=8), gastric (n=5), and ovarian (n=3) cancer. Intraperitoneal delivery achieved high local CAR-T concentration with minimal systemic exposure. Peritoneal disease response (CT + peritoneal lavage cytology): 44% (7/16). Ascites reduction in 63%. Systemic CRS was minimal (grade 1 in 25%, no grade ≥2). Local peritoneal inflammation (abdominal pain, fever) in 69% — manageable. Demonstrates compartmentalized delivery can achieve solid tumor responses with favorable systemic safety.
outcome_summary: 44% peritoneal response, IP delivery, minimal systemic CRS

title: ROR1-Targeted CAR-T in Relapsed/Refractory CLL and Mantle Cell Lymphoma
text_summary: Phase I of ROR1-directed CAR-T at Fred Hutchinson in 20 patients: 12 R/R CLL, 8 R/R MCL. ROR1 is broadly expressed across multiple tumor types. CLL ORR 58% (7/12), MCL ORR 50% (4/8). Defined 1:1 CD4:CD8 composition. CRS 50% (grade ≥3: 5%). CAR-T persistence >6 months in 60% of responders. ROR1 expression maintained in all relapsed patients (no antigen escape). Well-tolerated with no on-target/off-tumor toxicity (ROR1 minimally expressed on normal tissue). Expansion to TNBC and NSCLC cohorts ongoing.
outcome_summary: CLL ORR 58%, MCL ORR 50%, persistence >6mo, no antigen escape

title: HER2-Targeted CAR-T in Recurrent/Refractory HER2-Positive Sarcoma
text_summary: Phase I/II at Baylor evaluating HER2-directed CAR-T in 19 patients with recurrent HER2+ sarcoma (osteosarcoma n=10, rhabdomyosarcoma n=4, Ewing sarcoma n=3, other n=2). No lymphodepletion conditioning. ORR 16% (3/19, all PR). Stable disease in 42% (8/19). Median OS 10.3 months. No cardiac toxicity (a critical concern given the fatal case in a prior HER2 CAR-T trial using different construct). CRS in 16% (all grade 1). Safe at doses up to 1e8/m2. HER2 CAR-T shown to be safe in sarcoma with low-level HER2 expression, unlike high-expression breast/lung cancers.
outcome_summary: ORR 16%, SD 42%, no cardiac toxicity, safe in sarcoma

title: c-MET-Targeted CAR-T with mRNA Electroporation for Breast Cancer
text_summary: Phase I evaluating transiently expressed c-MET CAR-T (mRNA electroporation, no viral vector) delivered intratumorally in 6 patients with metastatic breast cancer. mRNA approach enables transient CAR expression (24-72h peak, cleared by 7 days) for safety. All patients received intratumoral injection into accessible lesions. Injected lesion inflammation in 83%. Tumor necrosis on biopsy in 50%. No systemic CRS or off-target toxicity. Proof-of-concept for mRNA-based transient CAR-T as safe approach for solid tumor targets with normal tissue expression risk. Repeated dosing every 2 weeks feasible.
outcome_summary: Tumor necrosis 50%, mRNA transient expression, no systemic toxicity
